title: The Master of Oxford's Catechism
summary: The repository XML copies University of Michigan item CME00011, an encoding of Carl Horstmann's 1885 four-page edition of the fuller Harley MS 1304 text. Horstmann directly identifies Harley 1304, ff. 100r-102v, and points to Wuelcker's edition of the shorter Lansdowne MS 762 copy, now cataloged at ff. 3v-5r. Wright and Halliwell first printed that shorter copy in 1845. Modern scholarship edits Harley and Lansdowne in parallel. The available Internet Archive source for Horstmann is a complete scan of a 1965 reprint of Englische Studien 8, not a manuscript facsimile. Both British Library legacy digital-object links are currently unavailable, so no public complete-codex or selected-leaf image object was cached.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00011
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify the file as CME00011.
The CME submodule is a distinct repository; this manifest does not alter its source artifact.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00011
entities.description: A public digital-text item representing Horstmann's title, subtitle, four printed pages of the Harley text, footnotes, explicit, and byline with structural and typographic XML markup.

date_statements.label: repository XML structural review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD revision date documents review of this serialization, not necessarily the item's first online publication.

notes: The U-M collection describes its 2016-2018 additions generically as manually keyed and proofread from public-domain editions; no exact item-level production record was found.
The exact item route triggered an automated Cloudflare challenge during this review, while the collection browse page continued to identify and link CME00011.

entities.label: Horstmann, Questiones by-twene the Maister of Oxenford and his Clerke (1885)
entities.description: The immediate source edition for CME00011: a single-witness transcription of the fuller Harley MS 1304 version, printed as a short article in Englische Studien 8.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: The current British Library record misprints this as 1855; the volume title page is dated 1885.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., 'Questiones by-twene the Maister of Oxenford and his Clerke,' Englische Studien 8 (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1885), 284-287.
bibliographic.title: Questiones by-twene the Maister of Oxenford and his Clerke
bibliographic.publisher: Gebr. Henninger; distributed by Truebner & Co. and B. Westermann & Co.

notes: The article begins partway down p. 284 after a preceding contribution and ends partway down p. 287 before the next article.
Horstmann identifies Harley 1304 in the subtitle and cites Wuelcker's partly abbreviated Lansdowne 762 version in a footnote.
The article supplies no general editorial-method statement; it visibly uses italic type for many letter sequences, parentheses, ellipsis, and a footnote declaring one number unreadable.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 1304
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century English paper miscellany, with parchment additions and later binding, containing John Lydgate's Life of Our Lady on ff. 1r-99r, the fuller copy of the Master of Oxford's Catechism on ff. 100r-102v, and the ABC of Aristotle and other proverbs on ff. 103r-103v.

date_statements.label: current British Library catalog date
date_statements.value: Second half of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: The exact work is cataloged within the parent record at ff. 100r-102v; no separate component record is exposed.

physical_description.support: Paper; parchment f. 1*; additional paper flyleaves and pastedown
physical_description.extent: 104 folios, plus one unfoliated paper flyleaf at the beginning, two at the end, parchment f. 1*, and one unfoliated pastedown
physical_description.format: Codex; Gothic cursive (Secretary) script; post-1600 blind-stamped and tooled brown-leather binding
physical_description.dimensions: 200 x 285 mm; text space 100 x 175 mm
notes: The work occupies ff. 100r-102v.

A complete reproduction must retain covers or boards, spine if offered, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, cancelled-leaf evidence, every recto and verso, and adjacent works rather than only ff. 100r-102v.
The current catalog records England as origin.
The current record says the legacy digital images are unavailable. No complete public Presentation manifest, image inventory, PDF, or selected work-leaf images were found on 2026-07-11.
The failed public-image check does not establish that the British Library lacks internal photography, microfilm, or an orderable surrogate.

entities.label: British Library, Lansdowne MS 762
entities.description: A London Tudor commonplace book containing the shorter copy of the Master of Oxford's Catechism on ff. 3v-5r among civic, devotional, prophetic, historical, practical, and literary material.

date_statements.label: current whole-volume catalog date
date_statements.notes: The catalog's Start Date is 1509 and End Date is 1570, but its displayed Date Range is 1509-1564; this record preserves the catalog's narrower displayed range and narrative estimate.

date_statements.label: older exact-copy assignment
date_statements.value: Written in the reign of Henry V (1413-1422)
date_statements.notes: Wright and Halliwell explicitly assign the manuscript copy to Henry V's reign, and Wuelcker repeats that placement. It conflicts with current whole-volume cataloging and remains unresolved for ff. 3v-5r.

holding.notes: The exact work is cataloged within the parent record at ff. 3v-5r; no separate component record is exposed.

physical_description.support: Paper, with parchment at ff. 1r-4v, 29r-33v, 61r-67v, 86r-94v, and 98r-99v
notes: The work occupies ff. 3v-5r and therefore crosses the cataloged support boundary between parchment through f. 4v and paper at f. 5r.
A complete reproduction must retain covers or boards, spine if offered, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, and adjacent commonplace-book material rather than only ff. 3v-5r.
The current catalog gives London as origin for the enclosing manuscript.
The current record says the legacy digital images are unavailable. No complete public Presentation manifest, image inventory, PDF, or selected work-leaf images were found on 2026-07-11.
Horstmann calls this version partly abbreviated, but the exact stemmatic direction between the two surviving copies is not inferred here.

entities.label: Wright and Halliwell, The Master of Oxford's Catechism (1845)
entities.description: The earliest verified collected-volume publication of the work, printing the shorter Lansdowne MS 762 text in Reliquiae Antiquae volume 1.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: The inspected volume title page is dated 1845; any earlier part-issue date remains unresolved.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., 'The Master of Oxford's Catechism,' in Reliquiae Antiquae, vol. 1 (London: John Russell Smith, 1845), 230-232.
bibliographic.title: The Master of Oxford's Catechism

notes: The edition explicitly says it is from Lansdowne MS 762 and supplies the now-conventional catechism title.
Its statement that the manuscript was written in Henry V's reign is retained as an older assertion, not silently reconciled with the current British Library date.

entities.label: Wuelcker, Zwiegespraech zwischen einem Oxorder Lehrer und dessen Schueler (1879)
entities.description: A later lineated edition of the shorter Lansdowne MS 762 version in volume 2 of Wuelcker's Altenglisches Lesebuch.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Paul Wuelcker, ed., 'Zwiegespraech zwischen einem Oxorder Lehrer und dessen Schueler,' in Altenglisches Lesebuch, vol. 2 (Halle: Max Niemeyer, 1879), 191-194.
bibliographic.title: Zwiegespraech zwischen einem Oxorder Lehrer und dessen Schueler

entities.label: Sauer and Bauer, To Instruct and to Entertain (2023)
entities.description: A modern parallel critical edition that prints the Master of Oxford's Catechism from Harley 1304 and Lansdowne 762 alongside the Old English Prose Solomon and Saturn and a reconstructed Latin source.

bibliographic.citation: Hans Sauer and Alessia Bauer, eds., To Instruct and to Entertain: Medieval Didactic Dialogues, Middle English Texts 67 (Heidelberg: Universitaetsverlag Winter, 2023), lv + 144 pp.
bibliographic.title: To Instruct and to Entertain: Medieval Didactic Dialogues

notes: This later comparison edition is not upstream of Horstmann or CME00011.
The reconstructed Latin is an editorial hypothesis; no surviving Latin witness to that source is represented as a lineage entity here.

entities.label: Johnson Reprint Corporation reprint of Englische Studien 8 (1965)
entities.description: The first Johnson reprinting of the complete 1885 volume, retaining the original title page, contents, pagination, and Horstmann article while adding 1965 reprint statements.

bibliographic.citation: Englische Studien, vol. 8 (1885; first reprinting, New York and London: Johnson Reprint Corporation and Johnson Reprint Company Limited, 1965).
bibliographic.title: Englische Studien
bibliographic.publisher: Johnson Reprint Corporation; Johnson Reprint Company Limited

notes: The scan inspected for this manifest is of this reprint, not proof that U-M keyed from this exact physical copy.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the 1965 reprint of Englische Studien 8
entities.description: A complete 522-page PDF page-image scan of the reprinted bound volume, including front matter, Horstmann's article, adjacent contributions, and end matter.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR text layer
notes: Coverage is complete for the printed reprint volume. It is not a facsimile of Harley MS 1304.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Reliquiae Antiquae volume 1 (1845)
entities.description: A complete 350-page PDF page-image scan of the printed volume containing Wright and Halliwell's Lansdowne edition on pp. 230-232.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR text layer
notes: Coverage is complete for the printed volume. It is not a facsimile of Lansdowne MS 762.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Wuelcker's Altenglisches Lesebuch volume 2 (1879)
entities.description: A complete 441-page PDF page-image scan of the printed volume containing Wuelcker's Lansdowne edition on pp. 191-194.
notes: Coverage is complete for the printed volume. It is not a facsimile of Lansdowne MS 762.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Abbreviated or supplied letter sequences in the manuscript transcription
editorial_practices.representation: Many individual letters or short sequences are printed in italic type and retained as HI/@REND='italic' in CME00011
editorial_practices.description: The distribution of Horstmann's italic letters is consistent with editorial expansion, but the four-page article supplies no explicit global method statement. The interpretation is therefore recorded with medium confidence rather than converted into an unqualified manuscript fact.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A four-page article embedded within Englische Studien volume 8
editorial_practices.representation: One catechism division with article title, subtitle, text, footnotes, page milestones, explicit, and byline
editorial_practices.description: CME00011 encodes the complete target article but not the serial volume's title page, contents, preceding and following articles, or 1965 reprint matter.

open_questions.question: Can a complete public or repository-supplied photographic object of Harley MS 1304 be obtained and mapped to the work at ff. 100r-102v?
next_steps: Ask British Library Digitisation Services whether the legacy complete object can be restored or supplied and whether a new complete-codex order is feasible.
Specify covers or boards, spine if offered, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, cancelled-leaf evidence, and every recto and verso through f. 104; identify ff. 100r-102v only as the work mapping.
If a complete object becomes public, cache the entire deliverable or IIIF canvas sequence and record exact start/end canvases without cropping to the work.
Keep research access, reproduction supply, and publication permission separate.
notes: The current catalog says the legacy digital images are unavailable; both tested public IIIF routes also failed. No selected-leaf substitute was found.

open_questions.question: Can a complete image sequence and component-level codicological analysis of Lansdowne MS 762 resolve the exact date, support transition, and textual extent of the copy on ff. 3v-5r?
next_steps: Ask British Library Digitisation Services for the legacy complete object or a new complete-codex reproduction, including covers, endleaves, blanks, all additional leaves, and every recto and verso.
Map the work to ff. 3v-5r and inspect the parchment-to-paper boundary at 4v/5r, changes of hand, quire structure, inks, and any evidence for an inserted older unit.
Compare the physical evidence with Wright and Halliwell's Henry V claim and the current whole-volume 1509-1564 catalog date.
If only selected leaves are eventually released, cache permitted files as coverage=partial and retain the negative whole-object finding.

open_questions.question: Did U-M key CME00011 from an original 1885 copy, the 1965 Johnson reprint, or another reproduction, and what item-level correction history followed?
next_steps: Seek item-level U-M production metadata or a raw distribution package that identifies the physical or digital keying copy.
Treat the collection-level manual-keying statement and 2016 TEMPHEAD review as context until exact production evidence is available.
Continue to cite Horstmann's 1885 edition as the textual source while keeping the inspected 1965 reproduction layer separate.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME00011.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00011 through IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID; records a 2016 structural review; marks the text enm; and represents Horstmann's article title, Harley subtitle, two footnotes, fifty alternating exchanges, four printed-page breaks, italic typography, one illegibility gap, explicit, and byline.
Counts 1 DIV1, 100 SP, 100 SPEAKER, 121 P, 4 PB, 2 NOTE, and 1 GAP elements; the alternating speaker sequence yields fifty clerk/master exchanges.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00011 and collection browse citation.
evidence.locator: exact item link and browse entry
evidence.summary: The collection browse page identifies the anonymous Questiones item as Horstmann's Englische Studien 8 (1885) edition and links exact item CME00011.
notes: The exact item route returned an automated Cloudflare challenge during this review; identity is independently confirmed by the browse page and repository artifact.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection and About pages.
evidence.locator: collection history, production, XML availability, and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: States that later CME additions were manually keyed from public-domain editions, proofread and edited in house, and marked up with basic TEI semantics; provides bulk XML; and asserts public-domain status in the United States with No Copyright-United States.
notes: The production statement is collection-level, not an item-specific keying log.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., 'Questiones by-twene the Maister of Oxenford and his Clerke,' Englische Studien 8 (1885), 284-287, in the Johnson Reprint Corporation first reprinting (1965).
evidence.locator: original volume title page; contents; 1965 reprint statements; printed pp. 284-287 / IA leaves n294-n297
evidence.summary: The original volume title page establishes 1885. Horstmann names Harley 1304, prints the fuller text, cites Wuelcker's partly abbreviated Lansdowne version, flags one unreadable number, and signs the edition. The scanned object identifies itself as the first Johnson reprinting in 1965.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan-data XML for Englische Studien 1885, volume 8.
evidence.summary: Maps Horstmann's printed pp. 284-287 to Internet Archive leaves n294-n297 and supports the exact work-portion deep links.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 1304, record 040-002047133.
evidence.locator: contents; extent; digital-content status; date; access; physical characteristics; origin; publications
evidence.summary: Places the work at ff. 100r-102v in a 104-folio miscellany, dates the codex 1450-1499, describes its supports, dimensions, collation, script, binding, flyleaves and pastedown, assigns origin to England, provides onsite access, and labels the legacy digitized object unavailable.
notes: The publications list misdates Horstmann as 1855; the immediate edition title page establishes 1885.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Lansdowne MS 762, record 040-002077106.
evidence.locator: title; contents ff. 3v-5r; whole-volume date; access; physical characteristics; origin; digital-content status
evidence.summary: Places the Questions at ff. 3v-5r in a London Tudor commonplace book probably compiled in the 1520s or 1530s; gives date range 1509-1564, extent ff. 99 + 4, 230 x 185 mm dimensions, mixed paper/parchment supports, and a British Museum binding; and labels the legacy digitized object unavailable.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., Reliquiae Antiquae, vol. 1 (London: John Russell Smith, 1845), 230-232.
evidence.locator: title page; The Master of Oxford's Catechism, pp. 230-232 / IA leaves n244-n246
evidence.summary: Prints the shorter Lansdowne MS 762 copy, supplies the conventional catechism title, and asserts that the manuscript was written in Henry V's reign.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan-data XML for Reliquiae Antiquae, volume 1 (1845).
evidence.summary: Maps the edition's printed pp. 230-232 to Internet Archive leaves n244-n246 and supports the exact work-portion deep links.

evidence.citation: Richard Paul Wuelcker, ed., Altenglisches Lesebuch, vol. 2 (Halle: Max Niemeyer, 1879), 191-194.
evidence.locator: title page; item 44, printed pp. 191-194 / IA leaves n202-n205
evidence.summary: Prints and lineates the shorter Lansdowne 762 version, identifies the manuscript, places the item under Henry V, and supplies the exact edition later cited by Horstmann.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan-data XML for Wuelcker's Altenglisches Lesebuch.
evidence.locator: leaf sequence around n202-n205, verified against printed pp. 191-194 in the complete PDF
evidence.summary: Supports the complete scan's leaf sequence; visual PDF inspection maps the edition's printed pp. 191-194 to Internet Archive leaves n202-n205.

evidence.citation: The Medieval Review, review 08.12.01 of Form and Content of Instruction in Anglo-Saxon England (2007), discussing Hans Sauer, 'A Didactic Dialogue in Old and Middle English Versions.'
evidence.summary: Reports that Sauer treats the Prose Solomon and Saturn and Master of Oxford's Catechism in textual and intellectual context, calls their neglected connection nearly identical, and discusses contents, structure, language, transmission, and manuscript relations.

evidence.citation: Mary F. Wheaton, 'Chapter III. Middle English: 2. Manuscript and Technical Studies,' The Year's Work in English Studies (published 3 December 2025), with Christopher Blake Shedd's 2025 review of Sauer and Bauer as supplementary detail.
evidence.locator: extract on the first parallel edition and review description of the four-column text and apparatus
evidence.summary: Identifies Harley 1304 and Lansdowne 762 as the two Middle English copies edited in parallel, notes a tentative English-text stemma plus dating and dialect discussion, and describes the reconstructed Latin, Prose Solomon and Saturn, Harley, and Lansdowne columns with critical apparatus.

evidence.locator: collection-image ordering, required shelfmark information, manuscript photography, delivery, preservation review, and licensing contact
evidence.summary: Provides a route to request collection photography, requires exact shelfmarks and page or folio details, distinguishes reference from reproduction work, subjects manuscripts to preservation review, and directs reproduction-permission enquiries separately.

review_notes: The complete cached 1845, 1879, and 1965 printed-volume PDFs were opened and checked for identity, title pages, article boundaries, work-page mapping, and distinguishing reprint matter from original publication matter.
The cached scan-data XML files were used only as digital page maps. Their metadata_only coverage does not imply image coverage.
Whole-manuscript negative findings are dated and scoped to publicly obtainable files and IIIF routes. They do not assert that the British Library lacks internal, onsite, or orderable reproductions.
No manuscript image excerpt was substituted for a complete physical object, and no provider-restricted image bytes were downloaded.
